allies.rationale_text: Scaffolding supports synthesis-and-integration by connecting smaller units into a larger structure that people can actually hold and use.
Systems-thinking gives synthesis-and-integration the ability to turn dispersed observations into a coherent picture of interrelationships.
allies.source_quote: Systems thinking explicitly calls for synthesis to see interrelationships rather than static snapshots.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Synthesis-and-integration conflicts with decomposition when synthesizing components back together loses the diagnostic precision that decomposition achieved.
structured_tensions.source_quote: synthesis-and-integration conflicts with decomposition (decomposition) when synthesizing components back together loses the diagnostic precision that decomposition achieved.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Synthesis-and-integration conflicts with simplification when integrating multiple frameworks produces a composite that is more complex than any single model and harder to act on.
structured_tensions.source_quote: synthesis-and-integration conflicts with simplification (simplification) when integrating multiple frameworks produces a composite that is more complex than any single model and harder to act on.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the relation surface sparse around the two clearest support models rather than forcing weaker antagonists.
donor_drops: Did not preserve broader communication or data-analysis relation sprawl beyond the clearest current-vocabulary supports.
open_questions: Prioritization is source-adjacent, but the first-order surface is cleaner around scaffolding and systems-thinking.
